description: Relative conformer energies from high-level ab initio literature (and corroborating gas-phase experiment), for benchmarking an ML potential's ability to reproduce conformational energy differences. Each entry gives the energy of a higher conformer relative to the reference (global-minimum) conformer of the same molecule.

units.convention: delta_E = E(conformer) - E(reference conformer); positive means the conformer is higher in energy

caveat: Mind what each reference energy INCLUDES. Some are pure electronic (Born-Oppenheimer, no ZPE) differences at the CCSD(T)/CBS limit; others are ZPE-corrected or effective 0 K/298 K values inferred from spectroscopy. The 'energy_type' field states which. To compare an ML potential like-with-like: relax BOTH conformers with the potential and take the electronic-energy difference to compare against an electronic reference; add ZPE (from a Hessian) before comparing against a ZPE-corrected or experimental reference. Conformer geometries in geometries/ are specific rotamers/structures matching each entry.

source.paper: Gruzman, D.; Karton, A.; Martin, J. M. L. Performance of Ab Initio and Density Functional Methods for Conformational Equilibria of C_nH_2n+2 Alkane Isomers (n=4-8). J. Phys. Chem. A 2009, 113, 11974-11983.

entries.note: Experimentally corroborated: gas-phase enthalpy difference 0.67 +/- 0.10 kcal/mol (Raman, Balabin J. Phys. Chem. A 2009, doi:10.1021/jp809639s) and 0.69 +/- 0.10 (IR/Raman). Electron diffraction 0.71 +/- 0.02. The 0.611 electronic value and ~0.67 thermal value are consistent.

entries.energy_type: effective 0 K gap (~42 cm^-1); CCSD(T) electronic + spectroscopy
entries.level_of_theory: CCSD(T)/aug-cc-pVQZ and CCSD(T) PES; consistent with microwave/far-IR

source.paper: Kahn, K.; Bruice, T. C. Focal-point energies and the anti/gauche conformers of ethanol; and Boussessi, R. et al. Small Alcohols Revisited: CCSD(T) Relative Potential Energies. ACS Omega 2017, 2, 8834.

entries.note: trans (anti) is the global minimum; gauche is higher by ~40-47 cm^-1 (0.12-0.134 kcal/mol) across multiple CCSD(T) studies, matching the microwave/far-IR experimental gap of 41 cm^-1. Note: gauche is doubly degenerate (+/-), which affects populations even though it is slightly higher in energy.

entries.molecule: glycine (conformer II relative to conformer I)
entries.reference_conformer: conformer I (Ip; ttt; planar, bifurcated N-H...O=C, lowest energy)
entries.conformer: conformer II (IIn; ccc; O-H...N intramolecular H-bond, high dipole)

source.paper: Csaszar, A. G. Conformers of Gaseous Glycine. J. Am. Chem. Soc. 1992, 114, 9568-9575; and focal-point analysis, Chem. Phys. Lett. 2009, 477, 176.

entries.note: Conformer I is the global minimum; conformer II lies ~1.4 +/- 0.4 kcal/mol higher but has a much larger dipole moment and is the second conformer detected by microwave spectroscopy. CAUTION: glycine conformer ordering above conformer I is level-of-theory sensitive (II vs III near-degenerate at some levels); treat sub-kcal orderings carefully.

entries.molecule: L-cysteine (conformer II relative to conformer I)
entries.energy_type: electronic + corrections (equilibrium relative energy) at CCSD(T)-F12 benchmark
entries.level_of_theory: CCSD(T)-F12b/cc-pVQZ-F12 with core-correlation, relativistic, and post-triples corrections

source.paper: Benchmark Ab Initio Determination of the Conformers, Proton Affinities, and Gas-Phase Basicities of Cysteine. J. Phys. Chem. A 2022, 126, 9781.

entries.note: Benchmark: conformer I 0.00, II 1.53, III 1.60 kcal/mol. Corroborated by the focal-point study (Conformers of Gaseous Cysteine, J. Chem. Theory Comput. 2009, 5, 1511, doi:10.1021/ct900005c), which places three conformers within 6 kJ/mol (1.4 kcal/mol) and eleven within 10 kJ/mol. conformer_III_I datum (1.60 kcal/mol) available from the same benchmark.

entries.molecule: L-cysteine (conformer III relative to conformer I)
entries.energy_type: electronic + corrections (equilibrium relative energy) at CCSD(T)-F12 benchmark
entries.level_of_theory: CCSD(T)-F12b/cc-pVQZ-F12 with core-correlation, relativistic, and post-triples corrections

source.paper: Benchmark Ab Initio Determination of the Conformers, Proton Affinities, and Gas-Phase Basicities of Cysteine. J. Phys. Chem. A 2022, 126, 9781.

entries.note: Conformer II (1.53) and III (1.60) are near-degenerate; a demanding test of ML-potential energy resolution.

deferred.reason: Alanine dipeptide (Ac-Ala-NHMe) C5-vs-C7eq is a classic Ramachandran benchmark, but its relative energy is strongly level-of-theory dependent and no exact CCSD(T)/CBS value could be corroborated from a reachable primary source this session (Kang & Park, Chem. Phys. Lett. 2014, 600, 112, doi:10.1016/j.cplett.2014.03.038; Beachy et al., J. Am. Chem. Soc. 1997, 119, 5908, doi:10.1021/ja962310g are paywalled). Deferred to avoid guessing. Add once the primary table is obtained. C7eq is the accepted global minimum; C5 lies roughly 1-1.5 kcal/mol above it at correlated levels.
